title: Slow-cooker Irish stew
subtitle: Lamb, potato, and onion, cooked simple
excerpt: Lamb neck, potatoes, carrots, and onion slow-cooked in stock for seven to eight hours without browning, without flour, without complexity. The potato thickens the broth as it cooks and the lamb falls apart. A simple weeknight dish that needs nothing added to it.
sourceNotes: Traditional Irish stew is a peasant dish of lamb or mutton, potatoes, and onions — the core staples of 19th-century Irish subsistence. The original method requires no browning and no flour. The slow cooker is a near-perfect modern equivalent of the covered pot used historically. Carrots are a later addition, now standard. Lamb neck is the correct cut, though diced lamb shoulder works as a substitute.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well. The potato continues to break down on reheating, which thickens the broth further.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Reheat with a splash of extra stock — the potato absorbs liquid as it sits.

content.text: This is the simplest slow-cooker recipe in the collection. Everything goes in raw, nothing is browned beforehand, and the result is a broth-based stew where the potato does the thickening. Resist the urge to add flour or extra ingredients — the dish works precisely because it is left alone.

Layer the {{potato}} and {{onion}} in the bottom of the slow cooker. Add the {{lamb-neck}} and {{carrot}} on top. Tuck in the {{thyme-fresh}} and {{bay-leaves}}. Season with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}} and pour over the {{stock-chicken}}. The stock should just cover the ingredients — add a little water if needed. Cook on low for 7 to 8 hours, until the lamb is falling apart and the potato has begun to break down into the broth. Remove the {{thyme-fresh}} sprigs and {{bay-leaves}}. Taste and adjust seasoning before serving.

items.cause: Potato chunks too large and not dissolving
items.fix: Break a few of the potato chunks against the side of the pot with a spoon. The starch thickens the broth immediately

items.cause: Lamb neck needs the full cook time, especially for larger pieces
items.fix: Continue for another hour. Lamb neck is collagen-rich and only becomes tender once that collagen has fully dissolved

items.cause: Stock was weak or insufficient seasoning
items.fix: Season generously at the end. A good stock makes the difference in this recipe — use homemade or a good quality liquid stock rather than a cube

content.text: Irish stew is a winter weeknight dish. It needs no attention after the first five minutes of preparation. The slow cooker produces a result that is deeply flavoured and filling without any complication. Serve with soda bread to mop the broth.
